policy: Use these sources to cross-reference missing values. Do not synthesize rows when access is unavailable, licensing blocks extraction, or the source does not contain the needed fields.

sources.name: UEFA official match API
sources.notes: Already integrated; official source preferred for match metadata.

sources.name: ESPN soccer scoreboard and summary APIs
sources.notes: Already integrated; useful fallback for match events and stats.

sources.name: Football-Data.co.uk historical CSV files
sources.notes: Integrated for Premier League, La Liga, Ligue 1, Bundesliga, Serie A, and Primeira Liga from 2004-2005 through available 2025-2026 files. Used only for observed match-level rows and source-provided fields.

sources.name: BALLDONTLIE UEFA Champions League API
sources.notes: Requires API key. Candidate for filling player_match_stats without inference.

sources.name: football-data.org
sources.notes: Requires API key; useful for team/player cross-reference and schedules.

sources.name: openfootball champions-league
sources.notes: CC0 public-domain European competition data; useful for Champions-focused coverage validation and Europa League auxiliary training rows.

sources.name: openfootball leagues
sources.notes: CC0 registry lists cl, el, copa.l, and copa.s competitions. Good source for match-level auxiliary rows when parsed into the existing schema.

sources.name: openfootball south-america
sources.notes: CC0 public-domain source candidate for South American expansion. Use only observed match rows parsed from text/sqlite releases; do not infer player events from match scores.

sources.name: openfootball national teams
sources.notes: Public-domain source candidate for international competition fixtures and results. Integrate as auxiliary context while keeping Champions League as the core analytical scope.

sources.name: StatsBomb Open Data
sources.notes: High-quality open event data for selected competitions. Coverage is selective, so use as a traceable event-level donor where competitions/seasons overlap; respect StatsBomb attribution terms.

sources.name: FootyStats competition CSV/API
sources.notes: Potential source for Libertadores/Sudamericana player rows. Many downloads/API endpoints require registration or subscription, so ingestion must respect licensing.

sources.name: API-Football
sources.notes: Requires API key; broad historical coverage candidate.

sources.name: Statorium Champions League API
sources.notes: Commercial/requires credentials; useful for reliable match metadata.

sources.name: Data Sports Group soccer feeds
sources.notes: Commercial/requires credentials; very large coverage, but licensing must be respected.
